name: pygount

metadata.name: pygount
metadata.summary: count source lines of code (SLOC) using pygments

documentation:
[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/pygount)](https://pypi.org/project/pygount/)
[![Python Versions](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/pygount.svg)](https://www.python.org/downloads/)
[![Build Status](https://github.com/roskakori/pygount/actions/workflows/build.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/roskakori/pygount/actions/workflows/build.yml)
[![Test Coverage](https://img.shields.io/coveralls/github/roskakori/pygount)](https://coveralls.io/r/roskakori/pygount?branch=master)
[![Black](https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg)](https://github.com/psf/black)
[![License](https://img.shields.io/github/license/roskakori/pygount)](https://opensource.org/licenses/BSD-3-Clause)

# pygount

Pygount is a command line tool to scan folders for source code files and
count the number of source code lines in it. It is similar to tools like
[sloccount](https://www.dwheeler.com/sloccount/) and
[cloc](https://github.com/AlDanial/cloc) but uses the
[pygments](https://pygments.org/)
package to analyze the source code and consequently can analyze any
[programming language supported by pygments](https://pygments.org/languages/).

The name is a combination of pygments and count.

Pygount is open source and distributed under the
[BSD license](https://opensource.org/licenses/BSD-3-Clause). The source
code is available from https://github.com/roskakori/pygount.

## Quickstart

For installation run

```bash
$ pip install pygount
```

To get a list of line counts for a projects stored in a certain folder run for
example:

```bash
$ pygount ~/projects/example
```

To limit the analysis to certain file types identified by their suffix:

```bash
$ pygount --suffix=cfg,py,yml ~/projects/example
```

To get a summary of each programming language with sum counts and percentage:

```bash
$ pygount --format=summary ~/projects/example
```

To analyze a remote git repository directly without having to clone it first:

```bash
$ pygount --format=summary https://github.com/roskakori/pygount.git
```

You can pass a specific revision at the end of the remote URL:

```bash
$ pygount --format=summary https://github.com/roskakori/pygount.git/v1.5.1
```

This example results in the following summary output:

```
┏━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━┳━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━┓
┃ Language         ┃ Files ┃     % ┃ Code ┃    % ┃ Comment ┃    % ┃
┡━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━╇━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━┩
│ Python           │    18 │  47.4 │ 2132 │ 63.6 │     418 │ 12.5 │
│ TOML             │     2 │   5.3 │ 1204 │ 82.7 │       1 │  0.1 │
│ reStructuredText │     9 │  23.7 │  566 │ 64.8 │       1 │  0.1 │
│ Markdown         │     3 │   7.9 │   53 │ 49.1 │       0 │  0.0 │
│ Batchfile        │     1 │   2.6 │   24 │ 68.6 │       1 │  2.9 │
│ Text only        │     2 │   5.3 │   24 │ 82.8 │       0 │  0.0 │
│ Bash             │     2 │   5.3 │   12 │ 80.0 │       3 │ 20.0 │
│ Makefile         │     1 │   2.6 │    9 │ 45.0 │       7 │ 35.0 │
├──────────────────┼───────┼───────┼──────┼──────┼─────────┼──────┤
│ Sum              │    38 │ 100.0 │ 4024 │ 68.4 │     431 │  7.3 │
└──────────────────┴───────┴───────┴──────┴──────┴─────────┴──────┘
```

Plenty of tools can post process SLOC information, for example the
[SLOCCount plug-in](https://wiki.jenkins-ci.org/display/JENKINS/SLOCCount+Plugin)
for the [Jenkins](https://jenkins.io/) continuous integration server.

A popular format for such tools is the XML format used by cloc, which pygount
also supports and can store in an output file:

```bash
$ pygount --format=cloc-xml --out=cloc.xml ~/projects/example
```

To get a short description of all available command line options use:

```bash
$ pygount --help
```

For more information and examples read the documentation chapter on
[Usage](https://pygount.readthedocs.io/en/latest/usage.html).

## Contributions

To report bugs, visit the
[issue tracker](https://github.com/roskakori/pygount/issues).

In case you want to play with the source code or contribute improvements, see
[CONTRIBUTING](https://pygount.readthedocs.io/en/latest/contributing.html).

## Version history

See [CHANGES](https://pygount.readthedocs.io/en/latest/changes.html).

modules.name: pygount
documentation: Pygount counts lines of source code using pygments lexers.

modules.name: pygount.xmldialect
documentation: Function to obtain the language dialect used by XML source code.

classes.name: pygount.xmldialect.SaxParserDone
documentation: Pseudo error to indicate that the Sax parser ist done.

modules.name: pygount.common
documentation: Common classes and functions for pygount.

functions.name: pygount.common.lines
documentation:
> Generator function to yield lines (delimited with '

') stored in

: `text`. This is useful when a regular expression should only match on a
  per-line basis in a memory efficient way.

functions.name: pygount.common.deprecated
documentation:
Decorator to mark functions as deprecated and log a warning in case it is called.

Source: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/2536307/decorators-in-the-python-standard-lib-deprecated-specifically

classes.name: pygount.common.Error
documentation: Error to indicate that something went wrong during a pygount run.

classes.name: pygount.common.OptionError
documentation:
Error to indicate that a value passed to a command line option must be
fixed.

modules.name: pygount.write
documentation: Writers to store the results of a pygount analysis.

classes.name: pygount.write.LineWriter
documentation: Writer that simply writes a line of text for each source code.

classes.name: pygount.write.ClocXmlWriter
documentation:
Writer that writes XML output similar to cloc when called with options
--by-file --xml. This kind of output can be processed by Jenkins' SLOCCount
plug-in.

classes.name: pygount.write.SummaryWriter
documentation:
Writer to summarize the analysis per language in a format that can easily
be read by humans.

classes.name: pygount.write.JsonWriter
documentation: Writer JSON output, ideal for further automatic processing.

modules.name: pygount.lexers
documentation: Additional lexers for pygount that fill gaps left by {py:mod}`pygments`.

classes.name: pygount.lexers.IdlLexer
documentation:
Lexer for OMG Interface Definition Language (IDL) that simply uses the
existing Java lexer to find comments. While this is useless for syntax
highlighting it is good enough for counting lines.

classes.name: pygount.lexers.MinimalisticM4Lexer
documentation:
Minimalistic lexer for m4 macro processor that can distinguish between
comments and code. It does not recognize a redefined comment mark though.

classes.name: pygount.lexers.MinimalisticVBScriptLexer
documentation:
Minimalistic lexer for VBScript that can distinguish between comments and
code.

classes.name: pygount.lexers.MinimalisticWebFocusLexer
documentation:
Minimalistic lexer for WebFOCUS that can distinguish between comments and
code.

classes.name: pygount.lexers.PlainTextLexer
documentation:
Simple lexer for plain text that treats every line with non white space
characters as {py:data}`pygments.Token.Comment.Single` and only lines
that are empty or contain only white space as
{py:data}`pygments.Token.Text`.

This way, plaint text files count as documentation.

modules.name: pygount.summary
documentation: Summaries of analyses of multiple source codes.

classes.name: pygount.summary.LanguageSummary

methods.name: pygount.summary.LanguageSummary.language
documentation: the language to be summarized

methods.name: pygount.summary.LanguageSummary.code_count
documentation: sum lines of code for this language

methods.name: pygount.summary.LanguageSummary.code_percentage
documentation: percentage of lines containing code for this language across entire project

methods.name: pygount.summary.LanguageSummary.documentation_count
documentation: sum lines of documentation for this language

methods.name: pygount.summary.LanguageSummary.documentation_percentage
documentation: percentage of lines containing documentation for this language across entire project

methods.name: pygount.summary.LanguageSummary.empty_count
documentation: sum empty lines for this language

methods.name: pygount.summary.LanguageSummary.empty_percentage
documentation: percentage of empty lines for this language across entire project

methods.name: pygount.summary.LanguageSummary.file_count
documentation: number of source code files for this language

methods.name: pygount.summary.LanguageSummary.file_percentage
documentation: percentage of files in project

methods.name: pygount.summary.LanguageSummary.line_count
documentation: sum count of all lines of any kind for this language

methods.name: pygount.summary.LanguageSummary.string_count
documentation: sum number of lines containing strings for this language

methods.name: pygount.summary.LanguageSummary.string_percentage
documentation: percentage of lines containing strings for this language across entire project

methods.name: pygount.summary.LanguageSummary.source_count
documentation: sum number of source lines of code

methods.name: pygount.summary.LanguageSummary.source_percentage
documentation: percentage of source lines for code for this language across entire project

methods.name: pygount.summary.LanguageSummary.is_pseudo_language
documentation: ``True`` if the language is not a real programming language

methods.name: pygount.summary.LanguageSummary.sort_key
documentation: sort key to sort multiple languages by importance

methods.name: pygount.summary.LanguageSummary.add
documentation: Add counts from `source_analysis` to total counts for this language.
Summary of a source code counts from multiple files of the same language.

classes.name: pygount.summary.ProjectSummary

methods.name: pygount.summary.ProjectSummary.language_to_language_summary_map
documentation: A map containing summarized counts for each language added with {py:meth}`add()` so far.

methods.name: pygount.summary.ProjectSummary.add
documentation: Add counts from `source_analysis` to total counts.

methods.name: pygount.summary.ProjectSummary.update_file_percentages
documentation: Update percentages for all languages part of the project.
Summary of source code counts for several languages and files.

modules.name: pygount.command
documentation: Command line interface for pygount.

functions.name: pygount.command._check_encoding
documentation:
Check that `encoding` is a valid Python encoding
\:param name: name under which the encoding is known to the user, e.g. 'default encoding'
\:param encoding_to_check: name of the encoding to check, e.g. 'utf-8'
\:param source: source where the encoding has been set, e.g. option name
\:raise pygount.common.OptionError if `encoding` is not a valid Python encoding

classes.name: pygount.command.Command
documentation:
Command interface for pygount, where options starting with defaults can
gradually be set and finally {py:meth}`execute()`.

modules.name: pygount.analysis
documentation: Functions to analyze source code and count lines in it.

functions.name: pygount.analysis.matching_number_line_and_regex
documentation:
The first line and its number (starting with 0) in the source code that
indicated that the source code is generated.
\:param source_lines: lines of text to scan
\:param generated_regexes: regular expressions a line must match to indicate

> the source code is generated.

```
param max_line_count

maximum number of lines to scan

return

a tuple of the form (number, line, regex) or None if the
source lines do not match any generated_regexes.
```

functions.name: pygount.analysis.white_characters
documentation:
Characters that count as white space if they are the only characters in a
line.

functions.name: pygount.analysis.white_code_words
documentation: Words that do not count as code if it is the only word in a line.

functions.name: pygount.analysis._pythonized_comments
documentation: Similar to tokens but converts strings after a colon (`:`) to comments.

functions.name: pygount.analysis.encoding_for
documentation:
The encoding used by the text file stored in `source_path`.

The algorithm used is:

- If `encoding` is `'automatic`, attempt the following:

  1. Check BOM for UTF-8, UTF-16 and UTF-32.
  2. Look for XML prolog or magic heading like `# -*- coding: cp1252 -*-`
  3. Read the file using UTF-8.
  4. If all this fails, use the `fallback_encoding` and ignore any
     further encoding errors.

- If `encoding` is `'chardet` use {mod}`chardet` to obtain the encoding.

- For any other `encoding` simply use the specified value.

functions.name: pygount.analysis.has_lexer
documentation:
Initial quick check if there is a lexer for `source_path`. This removes
the need for calling {py:func}`pygments.lexers.guess_lexer_for_filename()`
which fully reads the source file.

classes.name: pygount.analysis.SourceState
documentation: Possible values for {py:attr}`SourceAnalysis.state`.

classes.name: pygount.analysis.DuplicatePool

methods.name: pygount.analysis.DuplicatePool.duplicate_path
documentation:
Path to a duplicate for `source_path` or `None` if no duplicate exists.

Internally information is stored to identify possible future duplicates of
`source_path`.
A pool that collects information about potential duplicate files.

classes.name: pygount.analysis.SourceAnalysis

methods.name: pygount.analysis.SourceAnalysis.from_state
documentation:
Factory method to create a {py:class}`SourceAnalysis` with all counts
set to 0 and everything else according to the specified parameters.

methods.name: pygount.analysis.SourceAnalysis.from_file
documentation:
Factory method to create a {py:class}`SourceAnalysis` by analyzing
the source code in `source_path` or the open file `file_handle`.

```
param source_path

path to source code to analyze

param group

name of a logical group the source code belongs to, e.g. a
package.

param encoding

encoding according to 

param fallback_encoding

fallback encoding according to


param generated_regexes

list of regular expression that if found within the first few lines
if a source code identify is as generated source code for which SLOC should not be counted

param duplicate_pool

a  where information about possible duplicates is
collected, or None if possible duplicates should be counted multiple times.

param file_handle

a file-like object, or None to read and open the file from
source_path. If the file is open in text mode, it must be opened with the correct
encoding.

param merge_embedded_language

If pygments detects a base and embedded language, the source
code counts towards the base language. For example: "JavaScript+Lasso" counts as
"JavaScript".
```

methods.name: pygount.analysis.SourceAnalysis.language
documentation:
The programming language the analyzed source code is written in; if
{py:attr}`state` does not equal {py:attr}`SourceState.analyzed` this
will be a pseudo language.

methods.name: pygount.analysis.SourceAnalysis.group
documentation:
Group the source code belongs to; this can be any text useful to group
the files later on. It is perfectly valid to put all files in the same
group.

(Note: this property is mostly there for compatibility with the
original SLOCCount.)

methods.name: pygount.analysis.SourceAnalysis.code_count
documentation: number of lines containing code

methods.name: pygount.analysis.SourceAnalysis.documentation_count
documentation: number of lines containing documentation (resp. comments)

methods.name: pygount.analysis.SourceAnalysis.empty_count
documentation:
number of empty lines, including lines containing only white space,
white characters or white code words

See also: {py:func}`white_characters`, {py:func}`white_code_words`

methods.name: pygount.analysis.SourceAnalysis.line_count
documentation:
number of total lines, which is what you text editor a `wc -l`
would show

methods.name: pygount.analysis.SourceAnalysis.string_count
documentation: number of lines containing only strings but no other code

methods.name: pygount.analysis.SourceAnalysis.source_count
documentation: number of source lines of code (the sum of code_count and string_count)

methods.name: pygount.analysis.SourceAnalysis.state
documentation: The state of the analysis after parsing the source file.

methods.name: pygount.analysis.SourceAnalysis.state_info
documentation:
Possible additional information about {py:attr}`state`:

- {py:attr}`SourceState.duplicate`: path to the original source file
  the {py:attr}`path` is a duplicate of
- {py:attr}`SourceState.error`: the {py:exc}`Exception` causing the
  error
- {py:attr}`SourceState.generated`: a human-readable explanation why
  the file is considered to be generated

methods.name: pygount.analysis.SourceAnalysis.is_countable
documentation: ``True`` if source counts can be counted towards a total.

Results from analyzing a source path.

Prefer the factory methods {py:meth}`from_file()` and {py:meth}`from_state` to
calling the constructor.

classes.name: pygount.analysis.SourceScanner

methods.name: pygount.analysis.SourceScanner.source_paths
documentation: Paths to source code files matching all the conditions for this scanner.
Scanner for source code files matching certain conditions.
